title: My English World: Grade 2 Capstone Game Zone
source: Generated from approved unit runtime and linked master dictionary

games.title: Meaning Match
games.description: Match each unit word to its approved child-friendly meaning.

rounds.prompt: Which word means: The word that a person is called.
rounds.explanation: name: The word that a person is called.

rounds.prompt: Which word means: A person whose job is to clean windows.
rounds.explanation: window cleaner: A person whose job is to clean windows.

rounds.prompt: Which word means: The part at the end of your arm, with fingers.
rounds.explanation: hand: The part at the end of your arm, with fingers.

games.title: Spelling Builder
games.description: Choose letter tiles to build important unit words.

rounds.prompt: Build the word that matches the clue.
rounds.clue: A person who lives near you.

rounds.prompt: Build the word that matches the clue.
rounds.clue: A person you work or play with.

rounds.prompt: Build the word that matches the clue.
rounds.clue: The star that gives Earth light and heat in the day.

games.title: Sentence Puzzle
games.description: Put unit words into the correct sentence order.

rounds.prompt: Build the complete model sentence.
rounds.answer: My name is Samira. What is your name?

rounds.prompt: Build the complete model sentence.
rounds.answer: Planting trees helps clean the air and gives us shade.

rounds.prompt: Build the complete model sentence.
rounds.answer: Twenty children are playing in the field.

games.title: Language Choice
games.description: Choose the form that makes the unit language accurate.

rounds.prompt: Which sentence describes the past?
rounds.explanation: Wrote tells about a completed past action.

rounds.prompt: Which sentence states a future goal?
rounds.explanation: Will expresses a future action or goal.

rounds.prompt: What is the best feedback?
choices: Your details are clear; add punctuation to the last sentence.
rounds.answer: Your details are clear; add punctuation to the last sentence.
rounds.explanation: Useful feedback is kind, specific and actionable.

games.title: Reading Detective
games.description: Use unit evidence to solve each reading clue.

rounds.prompt: What six earlier pieces did Samira find in her folder?
choices: Her welcome card, neighbourhood poem, firefighter drawing, sky journal, measurement table, garden promise and city map are named;...
She introduced herself and wrote about a neighbour, healthy habit, plant, home and city place, using pictures, labels and...
Use the kind, specific suggestion to improve the project.
rounds.answer: Her welcome card, neighbourhood poem, firefighter drawing, sky journal, measurement table, garden promise and city map are named;...
rounds.explanation: Review the unit text evidence: Her welcome card, neighbourhood poem, firefighter drawing, sky journal, measurement table, garden promise and city map are named;...

rounds.prompt: What was Samira's next English goal?
choices: She introduced herself and wrote about a neighbour, healthy habit, plant, home and city place, using pictures, labels and...
Reading aloud helps them notice unclear language, missing words and punctuation before submitting.
She wanted to read longer stories by herself.
rounds.answer: She wanted to read longer stories by herself.
rounds.explanation: Review the unit text evidence: She wanted to read longer stories by herself.

rounds.prompt: Why should learners read every page aloud?
choices: Her plant page, because she used labels and complete sentences.
Reading aloud helps them notice unclear language, missing words and punctuation before submitting.
She remembered to breathe and speak slowly.
rounds.answer: Reading aloud helps them notice unclear language, missing words and punctuation before submitting.
rounds.explanation: Review the unit text evidence: Reading aloud helps them notice unclear language, missing words and punctuation before submitting.

games.title: Speaking Quest
games.description: Listen, record, review and submit useful unit language.

rounds.prompt: Speaking 1 - Introduce My English World
rounds.target: Give a 30-second introduction with your name, project title and the four earlier units you selected.

rounds.prompt: Speaking 3 - Read Aloud with Expression
rounds.target: Planting trees helps clean the air and gives us shade.

rounds.prompt: Speaking 5 - Rehearse and Answer Questions
rounds.target: Give a two-minute rehearsal, then answer one What question and one How question from a partner.

games.title: Word Order Race
games.description: Arrange unit language in a clear, accurate order.

rounds.prompt: Put this unit sentence in order.
rounds.answer: I wash my hands before I eat.

rounds.prompt: Put this unit sentence in order.
rounds.answer: Can you spell your name for me, please?

rounds.prompt: Put this unit sentence in order.
rounds.answer: Use there are for more than one thing.

games.title: Definition Dash
games.description: Choose the approved meaning before moving to the next clue.

rounds.prompt: What does 'light' mean in this unit?
choices: The brightness that lets us see things.
The word that a person is called.
The top part of your body, with your eyes, ears, nose and mouth.
rounds.answer: The brightness that lets us see things.
rounds.explanation: light: The brightness that lets us see things.

rounds.prompt: What does 'thirty' mean in this unit?
choices: The word that a person is called.
The top part of your body, with your eyes, ears, nose and mouth.
rounds.explanation: thirty: The number 30.

rounds.prompt: What does 'head' mean in this unit?
choices: An insect with large, colourful wings that flies from flower to flower.
The top part of your body, with your eyes, ears, nose and mouth.
The word that a person is called.
rounds.answer: The top part of your body, with your eyes, ears, nose and mouth.
rounds.explanation: head: The top part of your body, with your eyes, ears, nose and mouth.

games.title: Word Type Power
games.description: Identify how each vocabulary word works in English.

rounds.prompt: What type of word is 'shopping center'?
rounds.explanation: shopping center is used as a noun in this dictionary sense.

rounds.prompt: What type of word is 'spell'?
rounds.explanation: spell is used as a verb in this dictionary sense.

rounds.prompt: What type of word is 'house'?
rounds.explanation: house is used as a noun in this dictionary sense.

games.title: Memory Pairs
games.description: Reveal tiles and connect each word with its meaning.

rounds.prompt: Match vocabulary set 1.
pairs: The top part of your body, with your eyes, ears, nose and mouth.
An insect with large, colourful wings that flies from flower to flower.
A building where books are kept to read or borrow.

rounds.prompt: Match vocabulary set 2.
pairs: The brightness that lets us see things.
Giving water to plants to help them grow.
To enjoy something or think it is good.

rounds.prompt: Match vocabulary set 3.
pairs: A small, simple house with only one or two rooms.
A person who finds out news and writes stories about it.

games.title: Question Quest
games.description: Read each unit question carefully and choose the best answer.

rounds.prompt: There ___ three pictures beside the booklet.
rounds.explanation: Use there are for more than one thing.

rounds.prompt: Which sentence gives a correct comparison?
choices: The metre is longer than the centimetre.
The metre is long than the centimetre.
rounds.answer: The metre is longer than the centimetre.
rounds.explanation: Longer than is the correct comparison form.

rounds.prompt: Which sentence describes an action happening now?
rounds.explanation: Is plus reading describes an action happening now.

games.title: Unit Mission
games.description: Finish with a mixed challenge across the whole unit.

rounds.prompt: This is Samira. ___ is presenting her booklet.
rounds.explanation: Use she for one girl or woman.

rounds.prompt: The learners ___ listening carefully.
rounds.explanation: Use are with the plural subject learners.

rounds.prompt: Which question asks about a person?
rounds.explanation: Who asks about a person.
